types/string-or-array.schema.json.$comment:
singbox allows using single string for array types if there is only one element.

This type is to allow the behavior.

store.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

The default X509 trusted CA certificate list.

- `system` (default): System trusted CA certificates
- `mozilla`: Mozilla Included List with China CA certificates removed
- `none`: Empty list

certificate.description: The certificate line array to trust, in PEM format.

certificate_path.description:
The paths to certificates to trust, in PEM format.

Will be automatically reloaded if file modified.

certificate_directory_path.description:
The directory path to search for certificates to trust, in PEM format.

Will be automatically reloaded if file modified.

strategy.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Set domain strategy for this query.

disable_cache.description: Disable cache and save cache in this query.

client_subnet.description:
Append a `edns0-subnet` OPT extra record with the specified IP prefix to every query by default.

If value is an IP address instead of prefix, `/32` or `/128` will be appended automatically.

disable_cache.description: Disable cache and save cache in this query.

client_subnet.description: Append a `edns0-subnet` OPT extra record with the specified IP prefix to every query by default.

method.description:
Reject method.

- `default`: Reply with REFUSED.
- `drop`: Drop the request.

`default` will be used by default.

no_drop.description:
If not enabled, `method` will be temporarily overwritten to `drop` after 50 triggers in 30s.

Not available when `method` is set to drop.

rcode.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

The response code.

`NOERROR` will be used by default.

answer.description: List of text DNS record to respond as answers.

ns.description: List of text DNS record to respond as name servers.

extra.description: List of text DNS record to respond as extra records.

server.description: Deprecated in sing-box 1.11.0. Moved to DNS Rule Action.

disable_cache.description: Deprecated in sing-box 1.11.0. Moved to DNS Rule Action.

rewrite_ttl.description: Deprecated in sing-box 1.11.0. Moved to DNS Rule Action.

client_subnet.description: Deprecated in sing-box 1.11.0. Moved to DNS Rule Action.

ip_version.description:
4 (A DNS query) or 6 (AAAA DNS query).

Not limited if empty.

query_type.description: DNS query type. Values can be integers or type name strings.

auth_user.description: Username, see each inbound for details.

source_geoip.description:
Match source geoip.

Deprecated in sing-box 1.8.0.

source_ip_is_private.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.8.0

Match non-public source IP.

ip_cidr.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.9.0

Match IP CIDR with query response.

ip_is_private.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.9.0

Match private IP with query response.

ip_accept_any.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Match any IP with query response.

process_name.description:
> Only supported on Linux, Windows, and macOS.

Match process name.

process_path.description:
> Only supported on Linux, Windows, and macOS.

Match process path.

process_path_regex.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

> Only supported on Linux, Windows, and macOS.

Match process path using regular expression.

user.description:
> Only supported on Linux.

Match user name.

user_id.description:
> Only supported on Linux.

Match user id.

network_type.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.11.0

> Only supported in graphical clients on Android and Apple platforms.

Match network type.

Available values: `wifi`, `cellular`, `ethernet` and `other`.

network_is_expensive.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.11.0

> Only supported in graphical clients on Android and Apple platforms.

Match if network is considered Metered (on Android) or considered expensive.

network_is_constrained.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.11.0

> Only supported in graphical clients on Apple platforms.

Match if network is in Low Data Mode.

wifi_ssid.description:
> Only supported in graphical clients on Android and Apple platforms.

Match WiFi SSID.

wifi_bssid.description:
> Only supported in graphical clients on Android and Apple platforms.

Match WiFi BSSID.

rule_set_ipcidr_match_source.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.9.0

Deprecated in sing-box 1.10.0. Use `rule_set_ip_cidr_match_source` instead.

rule_set_ip_cidr_match_source.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Make `ip_cidr` rule items in rule-sets match the source IP.

rule_set_ip_cidr_accept_empty.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Make `ip_cidr` rules in rule-sets accept empty query response.

outbound.description:
Deprecated in sing-box 1.12.0.

Match outbound.

`any` can be used as a value to match any outbound.

action.description: See DNS Rule Actions for details.
See DNS Rule Actions for details.

address.description: The address of the dns server.

address_resolver.description: Required if address contains domain.Tag of a another server to resolve the domain name in the address.

address_strategy.description: The domain strategy for resolving the domain name in the address.

strategy.description: Default domain strategy for resolving the domain names.

detour.description: Tag of an outbound for connecting to the dns server.

client_subnet.description:
Append a `edns0-subnet` OPT extra record with the specified IP prefix to every query by default.

If value is an IP address instead of prefix, `/32` or `/128` will be appended automatically.

Can be overrides by `rules.[].client_subnet`.

Will overrides `dns.client_subnet`.

detour.description:
The tag of the upstream outbound.

If enabled, all other fields will be ignored.

bind_interface.description: The network interface to bind to.

inet4_bind_address.description: The IPv4 address to bind to.

inet6_bind_address.description: The IPv6 address to bind to.

routing_mark.description:
Set netfilter routing mark.

Integers (e.g. `1234`) and string hexadecimals (e.g. `"0x1234"`) are supported.

netns.description:
> Only supported on Linux.

Set network namespace, name or path.

connect_timeout.description:
Connect timeout, in golang's Duration format.

A duration string is a possibly signed sequence of
decimal numbers, each with optional fraction and a unit suffix,
such as "300ms", "-1.5h" or "2h45m".
Valid time units are "ns", "us" (or "µs"), "ms", "s", "m", "h".

domain_resolver.description:
Set domain resolver to use for resolving domain names.

This option uses the same format as the [route DNS rule action](/configuration/dns/rule_action/#route) without the `action` field.

Setting this option directly to a string is equivalent to setting `server` of this options.

| Outbound/Endpoints | Effected domains         |
|--------------------|--------------------------|
| `direct`           | Domain in request        | 
| others             | Domain in server address |

network_strategy.description:
Strategy for selecting network interfaces.

Available values:

- `default` (default): Connect to default network or networks specified in `network_type` sequentially.
- `hybrid`: Connect to all networks or networks specified in `network_type` concurrently.
- `fallback`: Connect to default network or preferred networks specified in `network_type` concurrently, and try fallback networks when unavailable or timeout.

For fallback, when preferred interfaces fails or times out,
it will enter a 15s fast fallback state (Connect to all preferred and fallback networks concurrently),
and exit immediately if preferred networks recover.

Conflicts with `bind_interface`, `inet4_bind_address` and `inet6_bind_address`.

network_type.description:
Network types to use when using `default` or `hybrid` network strategy or
preferred network types to use when using `fallback` network strategy.

Available values: `wifi`, `cellular`, `ethernet`, `other`.

Device's default network is used by default.

fallback_network_type.description:
Fallback network types when preferred networks are unavailable or timeout when using `fallback` network strategy.

All other networks expect preferred are used by default.

fallback_delay.description:
The length of time to wait before spawning a RFC 6555 Fast Fallback connection.

For `domain_strategy`, is the amount of time to wait for connection to succeed before assuming
that IPv4/IPv6 is misconfigured and falling back to other type of addresses.

For `network_strategy`, is the amount of time to wait for connection to succeed before falling
back to other interfaces.

Only take effect when `domain_strategy` or `network_strategy` is set.

`300ms` is used by default.

domain_strategy.description:
Available values: `prefer_ipv4`, `prefer_ipv6`, `ipv4_only`, `ipv6_only`.

If set, the requested domain name will be resolved to IP before connect.

| Outbound | Effected domains         | Fallback Value                            |
|----------|--------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| `direct` | Domain in request        | Take `inbound.domain_strategy` if not set | 
| others   | Domain in server address | /                                         |

path.description:
List of paths to hosts files.

`/etc/hosts` is used by default.

`C:\Windows\System32\Drivers\etc\hosts` is used by default on Windows.

server.description:
The address of the DNS server.

If domain name is used, `domain_resolver` must also be set to resolve IP address.

server_port.description:
The port of the DNS server.

`53` will be used by default.

server.description:
The address of the DNS server.

If domain name is used, `domain_resolver` must also be set to resolve IP address.

server_port.description:
The port of the DNS server.

`53` will be used by default.

disable_sni.description: Do not send server name in ClientHello.

server_name.description:
Used to verify the hostname on the returned certificates unless insecure is given.

It is also included in the client's handshake to support virtual hosting unless it is an IP address.

alpn.description: List of supported application level protocols, in order of preference.

min_version.description:
The minimum TLS version that is acceptable.

By default, TLS 1.2 is used when acting as a client.

max_version.description:
The maximum TLS version that is acceptable.

By default, the maximum version is currently TLS 1.3.

cipher_suites.description: A list of enabled TLS 1.0–1.2 cipher suites. The order of the list is ignored. Note that TLS 1.3 cipher suites are not configurable.

certificate.description: The server certificate line array, in PEM format.

certificate_path.description:
The path to the server certificate, in PEM format.

Will be automatically reloaded if file modified.

fragment.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Fragment TLS handshakes to bypass firewalls.

fragment_fallback_delay.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

The fallback value used when TLS segmentation cannot automatically determine the wait time.

`500ms` is used by default.

record_fragment.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Fragment TLS handshake into multiple TLS records to bypass firewalls.

config.description:
ECH configuration line array, in PEM format.

If empty, load from DNS will be attempted.

config_path.description:
The path to ECH configuration, in PEM format.

If empty, load from DNS will be attempted.

fingerprint.description:
uTLS fingerprint.

Chrome fingerprint will be used if empty.

public_key.description: Public key, generated by `sing-box generate reality-keypair`.

short_id.description: A hexadecimal string with zero to eight digits.

server.description:
The address of the DNS server.

If domain name is used, `domain_resolver` must also be set to resolve IP address.

server_port.description:
The port of the DNS server.

`853` will be used by default.

server.description:
The address of the DNS server.

If domain name is used, `domain_resolver` must also be set to resolve IP address.

server_port.description:
The port of the DNS server.

`853` will be used by default.

server.description:
The address of the DNS server.

If domain name is used, `domain_resolver` must also be set to resolve IP address.

server_port.description:
The port of the DNS server.

`443` will be used by default.

path.description:
The path of the DNS server.

`/dns-query` will be used by default.

headers.description: Additional headers to be sent to the DNS server.

server.description:
The address of the DNS server.

If domain name is used, `domain_resolver` must also be set to resolve IP address.

server_port.description:
The port of the DNS server.

`443` will be used by default.

path.description:
The path of the DNS server.

`/dns-query` will be used by default.

headers.description: Additional headers to be sent to the DNS server.

interface.description:
Interface name to listen on.

The default interface will be used by default.

endpoint.description: The tag of the Tailscale Endpoint.

accept_default_resolvers.description:
Indicates whether default DNS resolvers should be accepted for fallback queries in addition to MagicDNS.

If not enabled, `NXDOMAIN` will be returned for non-Tailscale domain queries.

service.description: The tag of the Resolved Service.

accept_default_resolvers.description:
Indicates whether the default DNS resolvers should be accepted for fallback queries in addition to matching domains.

Specifically, default DNS resolvers are DNS servers that have `SetLinkDefaultRoute` or `SetLinkDomains ~.` set.

If not enabled, `NXDOMAIN` will be returned for requests that do not match search or match domains.

type.description: The type of the DNS server.

tag.description: The tag of the DNS server.

final.description: Default dns server tag. The first server will be used if empty.

strategy.description: Default domain strategy for resolving the domain names. Take no effect if server.strategy is set.

independent_cache.description: Make each DNS server's cache independent for special purposes. If enabled, will slightly degrade performance.

cache_capacity.description:
LRU cache capacity.
Value less than 1024 will be ignored.

reverse_mapping.description:
Stores a reverse mapping of IP addresses after responding to a DNS query in order to provide domain names when routing.
Since this process relies on the act of resolving domain names by an application before making a request, it can be problematic in environments such as macOS, where DNS is proxied and cached by the system.

client_subnet.description:
Append a edns0-subnet OPT extra record with the specified IP prefix to every query by default.
If value is an IP address instead of prefix, /32 or /128 will be appended automatically.
Can be overrides by servers.[].client_subnet or rules.[].client_subnet.

system.description:
Use system interface.

Requires privilege and cannot conflict with exists system interfaces.

name.description: Custom interface name for system interface.

mtu.description:
WireGuard MTU.

`1408` will be used by default.

address.description: List of IP (v4 or v6) address prefixes to be assigned to the interface.

persistent_keepalive_interval.description:
WireGuard persistent keepalive interval, in seconds.

Disabled by default.

udp_timeout.description:
UDP NAT expiration time.

`5m` will be used by default.

workers.description:
WireGuard worker count.

CPU count is used by default.

state_directory.description:
The directory where the Tailscale state is stored.

`tailscale` is used by default.

auth_key.description: The auth key to create the node. If the node is already created (from state previously stored), then this field is not used.

control_url.description:
The coordination server URL.

`https://controlplane.tailscale.com` is used by default.

ephemeral.description: Indicates whether the instance should register as an Ephemeral node.

hostname.description:
The hostname of the node.

System hostname is used by default.

accept_routes.description: Indicates whether the node should accept routes advertised by other nodes.

exit_node.description: The exit node name or IP address to use.

exit_node_allow_lan_access.description: Indicates whether locally accessible subnets should be routed directly or via the exit node.

advertise_routes.description: CIDR prefixes to advertise into the Tailscale network as reachable through the current node.

advertise_exit_node.description: Indicates whether the node should advertise itself as an exit node.

udp_timeout.description:
UDP NAT expiration time.

`5m` will be used by default.

path.description:
Path to the cache file.

`cache.db` will be used if empty.

cache_id.description:
Identifier in the cache file.

If not empty, configuration specified data will use a separate store keyed by it.

store_fakeip.description: Store fakeip in the cache file.

store_rdrc.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.9.0

Store rejected DNS response cache in the cache file.

rdrc_timeout.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.9.0

Timeout of rejected DNS response cache.

`7d` is used by default.

external_controller.description: RESTful web API listening address. Clash API will be disabled if empty.

external_ui.description: A relative path to the configuration directory or an absolute path to a directory in which you put some static web resource.

external_ui_download_url.description: ZIP download URL for the external UI.

external_ui_download_detour.description: The tag of the outbound to download the external UI.

default_mode.description: Default mode in clash, `Rule` will be used if empty.

access_control_allow_origin.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

CORS allowed origins, `*` will be used if empty.

access_control_allow_private_network.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Allow access from private network.

listen.description: gRPC API listening address. V2Ray API will be disabled if empty.

bind_interface.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

The network interface to bind to.

routing_mark.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

> Only supported on Linux.

Set netfilter routing mark.

Integers (e.g. `1234`) and string hexadecimals (e.g. `"0x1234"`) are supported.

netns.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

> Only supported on Linux.

Set network namespace, name or path.

udp_timeout.description:
UDP NAT expiration time.

`5m` will be used by default.

detour.description:
If set, connections will be forwarded to the specified inbound.

Requires target inbound support.

sniff_override_destination.description:
Override the connection destination address with the sniffed domain.

Deprecated in sing-box 1.11.0.

sniff_timeout.description:
Timeout for sniffing.

`300ms` is used by default.

Deprecated in sing-box 1.11.0.

domain_strategy.description:
If set, the requested domain name will be resolved to IP before routing.

Deprecated in sing-box 1.11.0.

udp_disable_domain_unmapping.description:
If enabled, for UDP proxy requests addressed to a domain, the original packet address will be sent in the response instead of the mapped domain.

Deprecated in sing-box 1.11.0.

network.description:
Listen network, one of `tcp` `udp`.

Both if empty.

users.description:
SOCKS and HTTP users.

No authentication required if empty.

set_system_proxy.description:
> Only supported on Linux, Android, Windows, and macOS.

Automatically set system proxy configuration when start and clean up when stop.

users.description:
SOCKS users.

No authentication required if empty.

server_name.description:
Used to verify the hostname on the returned certificates unless insecure is given.

It is also included in the client's handshake to support virtual hosting unless it is an IP address.

alpn.description: List of supported application level protocols, in order of preference.

min_version.description:
The minimum TLS version that is acceptable.

By default, TLS 1.0 is used when acting as a server.

max_version.description:
The maximum TLS version that is acceptable.

By default, the maximum version is currently TLS 1.3.

cipher_suites.description: A list of enabled TLS 1.0–1.2 cipher suites. The order of the list is ignored. Note that TLS 1.3 cipher suites are not configurable.

certificate.description: The server certificate line array, in PEM format.

certificate_path.description:
The path to the server certificate, in PEM format.

Will be automatically reloaded if file modified.

key.description: The server private key line array, in PEM format.

key_path.description:
The path to the server private key, in PEM format.

Will be automatically reloaded if file modified.

domain.description:
List of domain.

ACME will be disabled if empty.

data_directory.description:
The directory to store ACME data.

`$XDG_DATA_HOME/certmagic|$HOME/.local/share/certmagic` will be used if empty.

default_server_name.description: Server name to use when choosing a certificate if the ClientHello's ServerName field is empty.

email.description: The email address to use when creating or selecting an existing ACME server account.

provider.description:
The ACME CA provider to use.

- `letsencrypt` (default): Let's Encrypt
- `zerossl`: ZeroSSL
- `https://...`: Custom

alternative_http_port.description: The alternate port to use for the ACME HTTP challenge.

alternative_tls_port.description: The alternate port to use for the ACME TLS-ALPN challenge.

external_account.description: EAB (External Account Binding) contains information necessary to bind or map an ACME account to some other account known by the CA.

key.description: ECH key line array, in PEM format.

key_path.description:
The path to ECH key, in PEM format.

Will be automatically reloaded if file modified.

handshake.description: Handshake server address and Dial Fields.

private_key.description: Private key, generated by `sing-box generate reality-keypair`.

short_id.description: A hexadecimal string with zero to eight digits.

max_time_difference.description:
The maximum time difference between the server and the client.

Check disabled if empty.

users.description:
HTTP users.

No authentication required if empty.

set_system_proxy.description:
> Only supported on Linux, Android, Windows, and macOS.

Automatically set system proxy configuration when start and clean up when stop.

enabled.description: Enable TCP Brutal congestion control algorithm.

padding.description: If enabled, non-padded connections will be rejected.

network.description:
Listen network, one of `tcp` `udp`.

Both if empty.

managed.description: Enable this when the inbound is managed by the SSM API for dynamic user.

host.description:
List of host domain.

The client will choose randomly and the server will verify if not empty.

path.description:
Path of HTTP request.

The server will verify.

method.description:
Method of HTTP request.

The server will verify if not empty.

headers.description:
Extra headers of HTTP request.

The server will write in response if not empty.

idle_timeout.description:
Idle timeout duration.

Zero is used by default.

ping_timeout.description:
Ping timeout duration.

The default timeout duration is 15 seconds.

path.description:
Path of HTTP request.

The server will verify.

headers.description:
Extra headers of HTTP request.

The server will write in response if not empty.

max_early_data.description: Allowed payload size is in the request. Enabled if not zero.

early_data_header_name.description:
Early data is sent in path instead of header by default.

To be compatible with Xray-core, set this to `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol`.

permit_without_stream.description:
If enabled, the client transport sends keepalive pings even with no active connections.

Disabled by default.

host.description:
Host domain.

The server will verify if not empty.

path.description:
Path of HTTP request.

The server will verify.

headers.description:
Extra headers of HTTP request.

The server will write in response if not empty.

alterId.description: 0 to disable legacy protocol, > 0 to enable legacy protocol.

fallback_for_alpn.description: Fallback server configuration for specified ALPN.

network.description:
Listen network, one of `tcp` `udp`.

Both if empty.

up.description:
Max upload bandwidth.

Format: `[Integer] [Unit]` e.g. `100 Mbps, 640 KBps, 2 Gbps`

down.description:
Max download bandwidth.

Format: `[Integer] [Unit]` e.g. `100 Mbps, 640 KBps, 2 Gbps`

recv_window_conn.description:
The QUIC stream-level flow control window for receiving data.

`15728640 (15 MB/s)` will be used if empty.

recv_window_client.description:
The QUIC connection-level flow control window for receiving data.

`67108864 (64 MB/s)` will be used if empty.

max_conn_client.description:
The maximum number of QUIC concurrent bidirectional streams that a peer is allowed to open.

`1024` will be used if empty.

version.description:
ShadowTLS protocol version.

1 is used by default.

password.description:
ShadowTLS password.

Only available in the ShadowTLS protocol 2.

users.description:
ShadowTLS users.

Only available in the ShadowTLS protocol 3.

handshake.description: Handshake server address and Dial Fields.

handshake_for_server_name.description:
Handshake server address for specific server name.

Only available in the ShadowTLS protocol 2/3.

strict_mode.description:
ShadowTLS strict mode.

Only available in the ShadowTLS protocol 3.

wildcard_sni.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

ShadowTLS wildcard SNI mode.

Only available in the ShadowTLS protocol 3.

congestion_control.description:
QUIC congestion control algorithm.

`cubic` is used by default.

auth_timeout.description:
How long the server should wait for the client to send the authentication command.

`3s` is used by default.

zero_rtt_handshake.description: Enable 0-RTT QUIC connection handshake on the client side.

heartbeat.description:
Interval for sending heartbeat packets for keeping the connection alive.

`10s` is used by default.

up_mbps.description:
Max upload bandwidth in Mbps.

Not limited if empty.

down_mbps.description:
Max download bandwidth in Mbps.

Not limited if empty.

ignore_client_bandwidth.description: Commands clients to use the BBR CC instead of Hysteria CC when bandwidth is not set.

oneOf.description: HTTP3 server behavior (URL string configuration) when authentication fails.

rewrite_host.description: Rewrite the `Host` header to the target URL.

brutal_debug.description: Enable debug information logging for Hysteria Brutal CC.

interface_name.description: Virtual device name, automatically selected if empty.

address.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

IPv4 and IPv6 prefix for the tun interface.

auto_route.description: Set the default route to the Tun.

iproute2_table_index.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Linux iproute2 table index generated by `auto_route`.

iproute2_rule_index.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Linux iproute2 rule start index generated by `auto_route`.

auto_redirect.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

> Only supported on Linux with `auto_route` enabled.

Improve TUN routing and performance using nftables.

auto_redirect_input_mark.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Connection input mark used by `auto_redirect`.

`0x2023` is used by default.

auto_redirect_output_mark.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Connection output mark used by `auto_redirect`.

`0x2024` is used by default.

auto_redirect_iproute2_fallback_rule_ind.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.18

Linux iproute2 fallback rule index generated by `auto_redirect`.

loopback_address.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Loopback addresses make TCP connections to the specified address connect to the source address.

strict_route.description: Enforce strict routing rules when `auto_route` is enabled.

route_address.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Use custom routes instead of default when `auto_route` is enabled.

route_exclude_address.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Exclude custom routes when `auto_route` is enabled.

route_address_set.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Add the destination IP CIDR rules in the specified rule-sets to the firewall.

route_exclude_address_set.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Add the destination IP CIDR rules in the specified rule-sets to the firewall for exclusion.

endpoint_independent_nat.description:
Enable endpoint-independent NAT.

Only available on the gvisor stack.

udp_timeout.description:
UDP NAT expiration time.

`5m` will be used by default.

stack.description:
TCP/IP stack.

- `system`: Perform L3 to L4 translation using the system network stack
- `gvisor`: Perform L3 to L4 translation using gVisor's virtual network stack
- `mixed`: Mixed `system` TCP stack and `gvisor` UDP stack

include_interface.description:
> Interface rules are only supported on Linux and require auto_route.

Limit interfaces in route.

include_uid.description:
> UID rules are only supported on Linux and require auto_route.

Limit users in route.

include_uid_range.description: Limit users in route, but in range.

exclude_uid_range.description: Exclude users in route, but in range.

include_android_user.description:
> Android user and package rules are only supported on Android and require auto_route.

Limit android users in route.

platform.description: Platform-specific settings, provided by client applications.

bypass_domain.description: Hostnames that bypass the HTTP proxy.

match_domain.description:
> Only supported in graphical clients on Apple platforms.

Hostnames that use the HTTP proxy.

network.description:
Listen network, one of `tcp` `udp`.

Both if empty.

disabled.description: Disable logging, no output after start.

output.description: Output file path. Will not write log to console after enable.

server_port.description:
NTP server port.

123 is used by default.

interval.description:
Time synchronization interval.

30 minutes is used by default.

override_address.description:
Deprecated in sing-box 1.11.0.

Override the connection destination address.

override_port.description:
Deprecated in sing-box 1.11.0.

Override the connection destination port.

shared/udp-over-tcp.schema.json.$comment: The structure can be replaced with a boolean value when the version is not specified.

enabled.description: Enable the UDP over TCP protocol.

version.description:
The protocol version, `1` or `2`.

2 is used by default.

The SOCKS version, one of `4` `4a` `5`.

SOCKS5 used by default.

network.description:
Enabled network.

One of `tcp` `udp`.

Both is enabled by default.

protocol.description:
Multiplex protocol.

- smux: https://github.com/xtaci/smux
- yamux: https://github.com/hashicorp/yamux
- h2mux: https://golang.org/x/net/http2

h2mux is used by default.

min_streams.description:
Minimum multiplexed streams in a connection before opening a new connection.

Conflict with `max_streams`.

max_streams.description:
Maximum multiplexed streams in a connection before opening a new connection.

Conflict with `max_connections` and `min_streams`.

padding.description:
Enable padding.

Requires sing-box server version 1.3-beta9 or later.

plugin.description:
Shadowsocks SIP003 plugin.

Only `obfs-local` and `v2ray-plugin` are supported.

network.description:
Enabled network.

One of `tcp` `udp`.

Both is enabled by default.

alter_id.description:
Alter ID.

0 = Use AEAD protocol
1 = Use legacy protocol

global_padding.description: Protocol parameter. Will waste traffic randomly if enabled.

authenticated_length.description: Protocol parameter. Enable length block encryption.

network.description:
Enabled network.

One of `tcp` `udp`.

Both is enabled by default.

Enabled network.

One of `tcp` `udp`.

Both is enabled by default.

interface_name.description: Custom interface name for system interface.

local_address.description: List of IP (v4 or v6) address prefixes to be assigned to the interface.

workers.description:
WireGuard worker count.

CPU count is used by default.

mtu.description:
WireGuard MTU.

1408 will be used if empty.

network.description:
Enabled network.

One of `tcp` `udp`.

Both is enabled by default.

server_ports.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Server port range list.

hop_interval.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Port hopping interval.

`30s` is used by default.

up.description:
Max upload bandwidth.

Format: `[Integer] [Unit]` e.g. `100 Mbps`

down.description:
Max download bandwidth.

Format: `[Integer] [Unit]` e.g. `100 Mbps`

recv_window_conn.description: The QUIC stream-level flow control window for receiving data.

recv_window.description: The QUIC connection-level flow control window for receiving data.

network.description:
Enabled network.

One of `tcp` `udp`.

Both is enabled by default.

Enabled network.

One of `tcp` `udp`.

Both is enabled by default.

packet_encoding.description: UDP packet encoding, xudp is used by default.

version.description:
ShadowTLS protocol version.

1 is used by default.

password.description:
Set password.

Only available in the ShadowTLS v2/v3 protocol.

congestion_control.description:
QUIC congestion control algorithm.

`cubic` is used by default.

udp_relay_mode.description:
UDP packet relay mode.

`native` is used by default.

udp_over_stream.description: UDP over TCP protocol for QUIC stream based UDP relay.

heartbeat.description:
Interval for sending heartbeat packets.

`10s` is used by default.

network.description:
Enabled network.

One of `tcp` `udp`.

Both is enabled by default.

server_ports.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.11.0

Server port range list.

hop_interval.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.11.0

Port hopping interval.

`30s` is used by default.

network.description:
Enabled network.

One of `tcp` `udp`.

Both is enabled by default.

brutal_debug.description: Enable debug information logging for Hysteria Brutal CC.

idle_session_check_interval.description:
Interval checking for idle sessions.

Default: 30s.

idle_session_timeout.description:
In the check, close sessions that have been idle for longer than this.

Default: 30s.

min_idle_session.description:
In the check, at least the first `n` idle sessions are kept open.

Default value: n=0

executable_path.description:
The path to the Tor executable.

Embedded Tor will be ignored if set.

extra_args.description: List of extra arguments passed to the Tor instance when started.

data_directory.description:
The data directory of Tor.

Each start will be very slow if not specified.

server_port.description:
Server port.

22 will be used if empty.

user.description:
SSH user.

root will be used if empty.

host_key.description:
Host key.

Accept any if empty.

client_version.description:
Client version.

Random version will be used if empty.

outbounds.description: List of outbound tags to select.

default.description: The default outbound tag. The first outbound will be used if empty.

interrupt_exist_connections.description: Interrupt existing connections when the selected outbound has changed.

outbounds.description: List of outbound tags to test.

url.description:
The URL to test.

`https://www.gstatic.com/generate_204` will be used if empty.

interval.description:
The test interval.

`3m` will be used if empty.

tolerance.description:
The test tolerance in milliseconds.

`50` will be used if empty.

idle_timeout.description:
The idle timeout.

`30m` will be used if empty.

interrupt_exist_connections.description: Interrupt existing connections when the selected outbound has changed.

query_type.description: DNS query type. Values can be integers or type name strings.

process_name.description:
> Only supported on Linux, Windows, and macOS.

Match process name.

process_path.description:
> Only supported on Linux, Windows, and macOS.

Match process path.

process_path_regex.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Match process path using regular expression.

network_is_expensive.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.11.0

Match if network is considered expensive.

network_is_constrained.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.11.0

Match if network is in Low Data Mode.

download_detour.description: Tag of the outbound to download rule-set.

update_interval.description:
Update interval of rule-set.

`1d` will be used if empty.

udp_connect.description: Connect UDP connection to the destination.

tls_fragment.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Fragment TLS handshakes to bypass firewalls.

tls_fragment_fallback_delay.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

The fallback value used when TLS segmentation cannot automatically determine the wait time.

tls_record_fragment.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Fragment TLS handshake into multiple TLS records.

no_drop.description: If not enabled, `method` will be temporarily overwritten to `drop` after 50 triggers in 30s.

sniffer.description:
Enabled sniffers.

All sniffers enabled by default.

timeout.description:
Timeout for sniffing.

`300ms` is used by default.

server.description: Specifies DNS server tag to use.

disable_cache.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Disable cache and save cache in this query.

rewrite_ttl.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Rewrite TTL in DNS responses.

client_subnet.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.12.0

Append a `edns0-subnet` OPT extra record.

outbound.description: Deprecated in sing-box 1.11.0. Moved to Rule Action.

auth_user.description: Username, see each inbound for details.

source_geoip.description:
Match source geoip.

Deprecated in sing-box 1.8.0.

source_ip_is_private.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.8.0

Match non-public source IP.

process_name.description:
> Only supported on Linux, Windows, and macOS.

Match process name.

process_path.description:
> Only supported on Linux, Windows, and macOS.

Match process path.

process_path_regex.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

> Only supported on Linux, Windows, and macOS.

Match process path using regular expression.

user.description:
> Only supported on Linux.

Match user name.

user_id.description:
> Only supported on Linux.

Match user id.

network_type.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.11.0

> Only supported in graphical clients on Android and Apple platforms.

Match network type.

network_is_expensive.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.11.0

Match if network is considered expensive.

network_is_constrained.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.11.0

Match if network is in Low Data Mode.

rule_set_ip_cidr_match_source.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.10.0

Make `ip_cidr` in rule-sets match the source IP.

final.description: Default outbound tag. The first outbound will be used if empty.

auto_detect_interface.description:
> Only supported on Linux, Windows and macOS.

Bind outbound connections to the default NIC by default to prevent routing loops under tun.

override_android_vpn.description:
> Only supported on Android.

Accept Android VPN as upstream NIC when `auto_detect_interface` enabled.

default_interface.description:
> Only supported on Linux, Windows and macOS.

Bind outbound connections to the specified NIC by default.

default_mark.description:
> Only supported on Linux.

Set routing mark by default.

default_fallback_network_type.description:
🆕 Since sing-box 1.11.0

Default fallback network types.

verify_client_endpoint.description: Tailscale endpoints tags to verify clients.

home.description: What to serve at the root path.

mesh_psk_file.description: Pre-shared key file for DERP mesh.

servers.description: A mapping Object from HTTP endpoints to Shadowsocks Inbound tags.

cache_path.description: If set, traffic and user state will be saved to the specified JSON file.
